title: Make the profile API match the frontend contract
description:
# Issue #73 - Profile page renders 'undefined' everywhere

The React profile page reads profile.id, profile.username, profile.email and profile.joined (see starter/app.js), but GET /api/profile/{id} returns different key names and the id as a string. Unknown users crash the endpoint with a 500.

**Documented contract:**
```json
{ \"id\": 1, \"username\": \"alice\", \"email\": \"alice@example.com\", \"joined\": \"2025-03-14\" }
```

**Acceptance criteria:**
- the response uses exactly the documented keys and types (id is an integer)
- unknown user ids return 404 with a JSON detail message
optimal_hint: Fix the backend, not the frontend: return {id (int), username, email, joined} and raise HTTPException(404, ...) for unknown users. Students who rename fields in app.js instead of fixing the API are fixing the wrong side of the contract.
